reforms.description: The 'Escuelas de calidad' program was launched to improve the quality of basic education in poor socio-economic areas by providing competitive grants for school improvement and encouraging local decision-making. Additionally, the decentralisation process has led to the introduction of managerial innovations in some states to better suit local needs, including a modern budgetary and payment information system to reduce corruption and improve financial operations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance educational quality and local governance, which can lead to improved human capital and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Both programs have been actively implemented to improve education and governance.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at improving education and governance.
reforms.source_quote: Escuelas de calidad was launched in 2001 to improve the quality of basic education in poor socio-economic areas by giving competitive grants for improvement and encouraging local decision making.

reforms.description: The introduction of a new fast-track system for start-ups (SARE) aims to reduce the complexity of administrative procedures, facilitating the creation of enterprises and significantly decreasing the time required to establish a business.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying administrative processes, the reform encourages entrepreneurship and innovation, which can boost economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fast-track system has been successfully implemented to support startups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in enhancing the startup ecosystem but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The new fast-track system for start ups (SARE) developed since 2002 is helping to reduce the complexity of administrative procedures with a view to facilitate creation of enterprises.

reforms.description: The government project 'Concrete actions to enhance competitiveness' was announced in early 2005, providing a specific list of measures required to lift competitiveness in Mexico, although it falls short of addressing structural reforms needing constitutional changes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The measures aim to improve the overall competitiveness of the economy, which can foster innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The project has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the initiative is significant, it does not represent a major reform due to its limited scope.
reforms.source_quote: The government project 'Concrete actions to enhance competitiveness', announced in early 2005, provides a list of measures required to lift competitiveness.

reforms.description: The Mexican government aims to double spending on R&D to about 1% of GDP, reflecting a commitment to enhance research and innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced its intention to double R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major commitment to enhancing R&D funding in Mexico.
reforms.source_quote: There are other announced policy priorities which would imply additional spending, for instance for investment in R&D (where the objective is to double spending on R&D to about 1% of GDP).

reforms.description: The implementation of the Seguro Popular program in 2004 aimed to provide health care insurance to all individuals, particularly targeting those without coverage, thus improving access to health services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to health services can enhance public health outcomes, which is essential for a productive workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The Seguro Popular program has been successfully implemented to provide health care insurance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in health care policy aimed at expanding coverage.
reforms.source_quote: The System for Social Protection in Health (SPSS) that came into effect in 2004 created a family insurance (Seguro Popular) to ensure that all individuals have access to health care insurance.
